summary: No wireframe exists for /admin/settings (route-without-wireframe, as flagged in BRIEF.json). Visual conformance (§3.2.1) could only be assessed against product/security sense, not a design spec.
reproduction: 1. Search design/screens/ for any admin-settings wireframe file.
2. Confirm BRIEF.json screen.wireframe_files is an empty array and coverage_gap = 'route-without-wireframe'.
expected: A wireframe file to compare the rendered /admin/settings screen against.
observed: No wireframe file exists anywhere in the repo for this route; brief confirms this is a known, pre-flagged coverage gap.

summary: POST /api/admin/settings with a syntactically-invalid (non-parseable) JSON body from an UNAUTHENTICATED caller returns 422 (JSON decode error) instead of 401. Body parsing is evaluated before the auth dependency resolves on this route for malformed-JSON payloads specifically. Not exploitable -- the 422 response only echoes generic parser diagnostics, no protected data -- and a well-formed-but-wrong-type body (e.g. {"signupEnabled":"not-a-bool"}) correctly returns 401 first. Reproduced twice (identical both times).
reproduction: 1. curl -X POST https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/admin/settings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d 'not-json-at-all' (no Authorization header) -> HTTP 422 {"detail":[{"type":"json_invalid",...}]}
2. Compare: curl -X POST .../api/admin/settings -d '{"signupEnabled":"not-a-bool-xyz"}' (no auth, valid JSON syntax) -> HTTP 401 {"detail":"Not authenticated"} as expected.
3. Re-run step 1 in a fresh terminal session -> identical 422 result.
expected: Every /admin/* request should be auth-gated first regardless of body shape, per the router's own docstring ('EVERY route depends on AdminUser... an anonymous caller gets 401').
observed: For syntactically-broken JSON specifically, FastAPI's request-body decoding raises 422 before the AdminUser/get_current_user dependency chain runs. No data disclosure results; this is standard FastAPI dependency-resolution ordering, not a code defect specific to this router.
